2606.21368.title: Graph-of-Differences: Anatomy-Structured Difference Alignment for Medical Image Re-Identification
2606.21368.paper_summary: Medical image re-identification (MedReID) enables longitudinal patient linkage but remains vulnerable to shortcut learning and often produces decisions that clinicians cannot audit against named anatomy. We propose Graph-of-Differences (GoD), which grounds identity comparisons in explicit anatomical structure. Each image is represented as an anatomy graph whose nodes correspond to named anatomical regions; given an image pair, soft node correspondence is established, and differences are computed over matched anatomy. A graph-level difference alignment objective ties these anatomy-matched differences to the global backbone difference, ensuring the retrieval signal is anchored in homologous structures rather than arbitrary spatial tokens. Explanations are defined over named graph nodes and quantitatively audited via node insertion/deletion tests, replacing unstable pixel heatmaps with verifiable structure-level evidence. On internal benchmarks, GoD improves Rank-1 by +7.1 pp on fundus and +3.1 pp on CXR over a strong frozen-backbone baseline, with further gains on zero-shot external transfers confirming that anatomy grounding improves both accuracy and generalization. Code is available at https://github.com/GenMI-Lab/GoD.git.
2606.21368.paper_summary_zh: 醫學影像重識別（MedReID）使得長期患者連結成為可能，但仍然容易受到捷徑學習的影響，並且經常產生臨床醫生無法根據命名解剖學進行審核的決策。我們提出了差異圖（GoD），它將身份比較基於明確的解剖結構。每個影像都被表示為一個解剖圖，其節點對應於命名的解剖區域；給定一對影像，建立軟節點對應，並計算匹配解剖上的差異。一個圖層級的差異對齊目標將這些解剖匹配的差異與全局骨幹差異相連，確保檢索信號基於同源結構而非任意空間標記。解釋是基於命名圖節點定義的，並通過節點插入/刪除測試進行定量審核，取代不穩定的像素熱圖，提供可驗證的結構層級證據。在內部基準測試中，GoD在強大的凍結骨幹基線上，對眼底影像提高了+7.1個百分點，對胸部X光影像提高了+3.1個百分點，並且在零樣本外部轉移中進一步獲得增益，證實了解剖學基礎改善了準確性和泛化能力。代碼可在 https://github.com/GenMI-Lab/GoD.git 獲得。
2606.21368.author: Nichula Wasalathilaka et.al.
2606.21368.authors: Nichula Wasalathilaka, Abhijit Das, Imran Razzak, Dwarikanath Mahapatra
